title: 1 4 Leibniz Rule
body:
1.4 LEIBNIZ’ RULE

                                                    Leibniz Rule

                        Z v(x)                  Z v(x)
                    d                                    ∂f                 dv            du
                                 f (x, t)dt =               dt + f (x, v(x)) − f (x, u(x)) .
                   dx    u(x)                    u(x)    ∂x                 dx            dx


Leibniz’ Rule deals with the problem of what happens when we differentiate integrals of
functions of several variables. We will motivate the rule with a simple example in Example
2 but first a little revision on integration theory:

                                      SOME BASIC INTEGRALS

                                                   xn+1
                                      Z
                                        xn dx =         + C for n 6= −1
                                                  n+1
                                              Z
                                                 1
                                                   dx = ln |x| + C
                                                 x
                                                          eax
                                              Z
                                                 eax dx =      +C
                                                           a
                                        Z
                                                            cos ax
                                            sin ax dx = −          +C
                                                              a
                                         Z
                                                          sin ax
                                              cos ax dx =         +C
                                                             a
                                         Z
                                                          tan ax
                                             sec2 ax dx =          +C
                                                              a

 Example 2 Find                             Z 2x
                                       d
                                                    x6 t2 dt
                                      dx    1
first directly and then using Leibniz’ theorem.




                                                                    F 24x8 − 2x5      F


The problem with the direct approach above is that often the original integral is difficult
or even impossible. Leibniz’ rule then becomes the only alternative!

                                                2
 It is perhaps best to remember Leibniz in terms of words rather than symbols. It then
runs:


                                        Z v(x)
                                    d
                                                 f (x, t)dt =
                                   dx    u(x)

             {bring the derivative inside the integral and make it partial}+
{replace variable of integration with the upper limit times the derivative of the upper} −
 {replace variable of integration with the lower limit times the derivative of the lower}.
